visa_types.name: Study Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant DHA-1738 FORM 8  (http://www.southafrica-newyork.net/consulate/forms/bi84.pdf)(To be filled in Black Ink Only) 
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least 2 clear pages. Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa. 
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate with signature and cover page.
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
Signed Introduction/Recommendation Letter from an employer, where applicable, with contact details, (Full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers) stating purpose and period of visit.  Signed email attachments and faxes are acceptable. 
A signed letter of invitation/confirmation of training/conference, etc, from the inviting host with full details of the Company’s authorized representative; physical address and phone numbers(landline number is compulsory). 
Sufficient funds in form of  ORIGINAL  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank.
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

 Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant DHA-1738 FORM 8  (http://www.southafrica-newyork.net/consulate/forms/bi84.pdf)(To be filled in Black Ink Only)

Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport.

To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Download Form
You must submit an application for a visa  DHA 84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form, the same can also be accessed by click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3pJXDOXUe3apaaMS60ehdV/34976f91ba413361ffb32ebb3bd84bb4/south-africa-visa-checklist-latest.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
          
Application form should be filled in BLACK INK only.

visa_types.documents_required:
Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
 Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only) 
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least /2 clear pages. Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa..
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate – with signature and cover page.  
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
If visiting family or friends, signed invitation letter from host with physical address and contactable phone numbers, certified identity document of host (if South African, National South African I.D.
 If foreigner, passport bio data page and permit)
Certification should not be older than 3 months.
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old). Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications.  
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
A statement /letter by applicant confirming the purpose and duration of the visit.  If Self-employed, submit a signed letter and attach documents of ownership.  If retired, please submit a written statement.  If a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
Sufficient funds in form of  ORIGINAL  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Sponsor’s Business/Tax Registration Documents.
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Marriage Certificate/Affidavit accompanied by a letter if one spouse is taking care of expenses. 
Accompanying Children 
In addition to the requirements for the specific purpose of travel, Accompanying Children under 18 years will be required to provide:
Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines. A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of  a  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution.
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

visa_types.name: Treaty Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/13DaVEUgE9oLong0NXx2N5/c326a1dd8b4dc1a6a68788874552ba6d/trety-visa.pdf) to download documents required checklist.
- A treaty visa may be issued to a foreigner conducting activities in the Republic in terms of an international agreement to which the Republic is a party.
- 	Submit a letter from the relevant organ of state involved in the agreement attesting to the 
- Nature of the programme and the treaty under which it is conducted
- Participation of the foreigner in the mentioned programme
- Type of activities the foreigner is expected to perform under the agreement and the duration of the project
- Accommodation of a foreigner
- Any other relevant details pertaining to the foreigner’s stay in the Republic
- A written undertaking by the sending or receiving organ of state accepting responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant and his or her accompanying dependent family members, should it become necessary.
- Duly completed application form signed by applicant (IN BLACK INK ONLY)
- Two current Passport Photographs (35x45)
- Police clearance in respect of each Country in which the applicant resided since the age of 18 years
- Medical and radiological report
- Valid Yellow fever Certificate
- Copy of Passport
- Copy of flight booking
- Processing of  visa takes a maximum of 8 weeks

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A treaty visas is an official document that gives permission to applicant to enter South Africa to participate in programs that have been agreed upon by South Africa and the visitors’ respective countries

TWO WAY MANDATORY COURIER FEES: KES 3,500.00
Depositors will indicate their passport numbers on the deposit slips, and the same to be indicated as narration/beneficiary for each deposit in the bank system.
The bank will then generate a deposit slip which will be presented to the VFS Mombasa office along with all other supporting documentation and Application Form(s). Please make 2 photocopies of the bank deposit slip as part of the documentation.

## Documents Required
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/13DaVEUgE9oLong0NXx2N5/c326a1dd8b4dc1a6a68788874552ba6d/trety-visa.pdf) to download documents required checklist.
- A treaty visa may be issued to a foreigner conducting activities in the Republic in terms of an international agreement to which the Republic is a party.
- 	Submit a letter from the relevant organ of state involved in the agreement attesting to the 
- Nature of the programme and the treaty under which it is conducted
- Participation of the foreigner in the mentioned programme
- Type of activities the foreigner is expected to perform under the agreement and the duration of the project
- Accommodation of a foreigner
- Any other relevant details pertaining to the foreigner’s stay in the Republic
- A written undertaking by the sending or receiving organ of state accepting responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant and his or her accompanying dependent family members, should it become necessary.
- Duly completed application form signed by applicant (IN BLACK INK ONLY)
- Two current Passport Photographs (35x45)
- Police clearance in respect of each Country in which the applicant resided since the age of 18 years
- Medical and radiological report
- Valid Yellow fever Certificate
- Copy of Passport
- Copy of flight booking
- Processing of  visa takes a maximum of 8 weeks

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown. 
	The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.) 
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes. 
 	Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind 
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows. 
The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined 
 	Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

You must submit an application for a visa 
DHA-1738 FORM 8 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/htoeWNamEIAJnLdzdJs1b/a3a256756f27b32795464ee1fc25c05f/1738-010716_form.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the Checklist.

An application for a general work visa shall be accompanied by-
Proof of acknowledgment (MANDATORY) from the Department of Labour confirming that-
- Despite a diligent search, the prospective employer has been unable to find a suitable citizen or permanent resident with qualifications or skills and experience equivalent to those of the applicant;
- The applicant has qualifications or proven skills and experience in line with the job offer;
- The salary and benefits of the applicant are not inferior to the average salary and benefits of citizens or permanent residents occupying similar positions in the Republic; and
- The contract of employment stipulating the conditions of employment and signed by both the employer and the applicant is in line with the labour standards in the Republic and is made conditional upon the general work visa being approved;(original copy )

Full particulars of the employer, including, where applicable, proof of registration of the business with the Commission on Intellectual Property and Companies (CIPC);
An undertaking by the employer to inform the Director-General should the applicant not comply with the provisions of the Act or conditions of the visa;
An undertaking by the employer to inform the Director-General upon the employee no longer being in the employ of such employer or when he or she is employed in a different capacity or role.
Forms DHA-1738 duly completed and signed by applicant
Two Passport photographs (current)
Police Clearance Certificate in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in respect of all Countries where person resided one year or longer. (Must not be older than 6 months at the time of its submission)
Medical and radiological report

visa_types.documents_required:
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6E4ptg1HOFu9WYpkUWrPTb/c901694220f61aac5e7ce5495bb26566/exchange-visa.pdf) to download the documents required checklist.
Exchange visa may be issued by the department by the department to a foreigner in a programme of cultural, economic or social exchange organised or administered by an organ of state, or a public higher education institution, in conjunction with an organ of a foreign state.
Specific requirements and documents to be submitted
- An applicant for an exchange visa in terms of section 22(a) OF THE Act shall- In the case of a learning institution in the Republic, in conjunction with a foreign education and training institution or a foreign state institution organising or administering the programme, submit a letter from-
- The Department of Basic Education or Higher Education and Training or a learning institution in the Republic confirming that it is responsible for organising or administering the existence of the programme, outlining the activities, terms and condition and duration thereof and accepting full responsibility for the student while he or she is in the Republic; and
- The foreign state institution or education and training institution confirming the particulars of the applicant, the applicant’s enrolment with the foreign education and training institution, and the date on which the programme shall commence.
- In the case of a programme of cultural, economic or social exchange in the republic
- In case of a programme of cultural, economic or social exchange, organised or administered by an organ or state or a learning institution, in conjunction with a foreign education and training institution or a foreign state institution , submit a letter from-
- The Organ of state or foreign education and training institution confirming the existence of the exchange programme or
- The foreign education and training institution confirming the enrolment of the applicant or the foreign state institution conducting the programme, as the case may be:
- An applicant for an exchange visa in terms of section 22(a) or (b) of the Act, shall submit-
- Proof of valid return air flight ticket or written undertaking by the organ of state, learning institution or employer accepting responsibility for the return or deportation costs of the applicant , as the case may be;
- Proof of medical cover for the duration of the exchange period with a medical scheme registered in terms of the Medical Schemes Act.
- Police clearance from Country of ordinary residence
- Medical and radiological report
Processing of  visa takes a maximum of 8 weeks

visa_types.name: Interviews
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only)  
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least /2 clear pages. Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa. 
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate with signature and cover page.
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
Signed Introduction/Recommendation Letter from an employer, where applicable, with contact details, (Full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers) stating purpose and period of visit.  Signed email attachments and faxes are acceptable. 
A signed letter of invitation/confirmation of training/conference, etc, from the inviting host with full details of the Company’s authorized representative; physical address and phone numbers(landline number is compulsory). 
Sufficient funds in form of ORIGINAL recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the bank.   
Accompanying Children 
In addition to the requirements for the specific purpose of travel, Accompanying Children under 18 years will be required to provide:
Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of a recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the bank.  
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

## Overview
Interview visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within South Africa. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in South Africa he/she will need to obtain a work permit.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Interviews ]
The fee mentioned is per applicant
Effective 01 April 2016, the South Africa Visa Application Centre in Nairobi will commence the use of M-Pesa services. 
Applicants will be required to load their fees (a deposit of both High Commission Visa Fees and VFS Service Charge Fees and upon confirmation of the amount by the VFS Counter Staff) and after completion of document checks, for the transfer to be done. The Kenyan Nationals are exempted from paying Visa Fee if the stay in South Africa is less than 30 days. If the stay in South Africa is over 30 days, then Visa fees mentioned in the above table is applicable.
Please Note:
- 	The visa fee in KES is subject to change without notice.
Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via M-Pesa. 
- There is no exemption for VFS Service charge.
The above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable. 
MOMBASA SOUTH AFRICA VISA APPLICANTS 
For applicants applying from MOMBASA please deposit your fees as below, upon confirmation from the Visa Centre – 
How to pay the Visa fees: 
- 	VFS Service Fees, Courier Fees and any other fee applicable to be paid in cash at the Absa Bank branch listed below before submitting your visa application. 
Account Number: 2027878647
Account Name: VFS KENYA LTD - SOUTH AFRICA HOLDING. 
Mombasa Branch, Off Nkurumah Road 
Please note, applications lodged in Mombasa will attract the fees below per person over and above any other fees applicable depending on your visa category:-
TWO WAY MANDATORY COURIER FEES: KES 3,500.00
Depositors will indicate their passport numbers on the deposit slips, and the same to be indicated as narration/beneficiary for each deposit in the bank system.
The bank will then generate a deposit slip which will be presented to the VFS Mombasa office along with all other supporting documentation and Application Form(s). Please make 2 photocopies of the bank deposit slip as part of the documentation.

## Documents Required
Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only)  
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least /2 clear pages. Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa. 
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate with signature and cover page.
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
Signed Introduction/Recommendation Letter from an employer, where applicable, with contact details, (Full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers) stating purpose and period of visit.  Signed email attachments and faxes are acceptable. 
A signed letter of invitation/confirmation of training/conference, etc, from the inviting host with full details of the Company’s authorized representative; physical address and phone numbers(landline number is compulsory). 
Sufficient funds in form of ORIGINAL recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the bank.   
Accompanying Children 
In addition to the requirements for the specific purpose of travel, Accompanying Children under 18 years will be required to provide:
Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of a recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the bank.  
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Download Form
You must submit an application for a visa  DHA 84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form, the same can also be accessed by click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2DqL7x1qcXLDohaDf37URm/d42d74c2ec9cf34b680317c119f33d9f/short-term-checklist.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
          
Application form should be filled in BLACK INK only.

visa_types.documents_required:
- Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3RgpuIY9JdVh5LOMSJhPBo/625ad069f9b41950e8fa10855ddf2d3b/New-Intra.pdf) to download documents required checklist.
An application for an Intra-Company Transfer Work Visa shall be accompanied by- 
-  the foreigner's contract of employment with the company abroad valid for a period of not less than six months; and 
-  a letter from- 
- the company abroad confirming that the foreigner shall be transferred to a branch, subsidiary or an affiliate of that company in the Republic; and
- the branch, subsidiary or an affiliate in the Republic confirming the transfer of
the foreigner and specifying the occupation and capacity in which that foreigner 
shall be employed.
- 	In terms of section 19(5) of the Act, the relevant employer shall ensure that- 
- a foreigner is only employed in the specific position for which the visa has been issued;
- the foreign employee will at all times comply with the provisions of the Act and conditions of his or her visa and undertakes to immediately notify the Director-General if the employee refuses to comply with the provisions of the Act or conditions of the visa; and
- a plan is developed for the transfer of skills to a South African citizen or permanent resident.
	Duly completed application form signed by the applicant
	Passport valid for no less than 30 days after the expiry of intended visit
- Payment of the prescribed fee
- A vaccination certificate, if required by the Act
- Police Clearance Certificate in respect of each Country in which the applicant resided since the age of 18 years
- Medical and radiological report
Processing of relatives visa takes a maximum of 8 weeks

Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of a recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the bank. 
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

You must submit an application for a visa  DHA 84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form, the same can also be accessed by click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2DqL7x1qcXLDohaDf37URm/d42d74c2ec9cf34b680317c119f33d9f/short-term-checklist.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
          
Application form should be filled in BLACK INK only.

An application for a general work visa shall be accompanied by-Proof of acknowledgment (MANDATORY) from the Department of Labour confirming that-
- Despite a diligent search, the prospective employer has been unable to find a suitable citizen or permanent resident with qualifications or skills and experience equivalent to those of the applicant;
- The applicant has qualifications or proven skills and experience in line with the job offer;
- The salary and benefits of the applicant are not inferior to the average salary and benefits of citizens or permanent residents occupying similar positions in the Republic; and
- The contract of employment stipulating the conditions of employment and signed by both the employer and the applicant is in line with the labour standards in the Republic and is made conditional upon the general work visa being approved;(original copy )
- Proof of qualifications evaluated by SAQA and translated by a sworn translator into one of the official languages of the Republic;
- Full particulars of the employer, including, where applicable, proof of registration of the business with the Commission on Intellectual Property and Companies (CIPC);
- An undertaking by the employer to inform the Director-General should the applicant not comply with the provisions of the Act or conditions of the visa;
- An undertaking by the employer to inform the Director-General upon the employee no longer being in the employ of such employer or when he or she is employed in a different capacity or role.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Download Form
You must submit an application for a visa  DHA 84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form, the same can also be accessed by click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2DqL7x1qcXLDohaDf37URm/d42d74c2ec9cf34b680317c119f33d9f/short-term-checklist.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
          
Application form should be filled in BLACK INK only.

visa_types.documents_required:
Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only)
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least 2 clear pages. Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa.
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate  with signature and cover page.
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
Signed Introduction/Recommendation Letter from an employer, where applicable, with contact details, (Full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers) stating purpose and period of visit.  Signed email attachments and faxes are acceptable. 
A signed letter of invitation/confirmation of training/conference, etc, from the inviting host with full details of the Company’s authorized representative; physical address and phone numbers(landline number is compulsory). 
Sufficient funds in form of   ORIGINAL recent bank statement for the last three(3) months  signed and stamped by the bank. 
Accompanying Children 
In addition to the requirements for the specific purpose of travel, Accompanying Children under 18 years will be required to provide:
Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines. A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of  a  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution.
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only) 
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least 2 clear pages . Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa. 
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate –  with signature and cover page.
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit the next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory.
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
Signed Introduction/Recommendation Letter from an employer, where applicable, with contact details, (Full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers) stating purpose and period of visit.  Signed email attachments and faxes are acceptable. 
A signed letter of invitation/confirmation of training/conference, etc, from the inviting host with full details of the Company’s authorized representative; physical address and phone numbers(landline number is compulsory). 
Sufficient funds in the form  of  ORIGINAL    bank statements for the last three(3) months signed  and stamped by the bank 
Accompanying Children 
In addition to the requirements for the specific purpose of travel, Accompanying Children under 18 years will be required to provide:
Parental Consent Affidavit – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of a  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so. 
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business Visa is required if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. E.g.; Meetings, Conference, Training Visas, Trade Fairs, etc; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within South Africa. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in South Africa he/she will need to obtain a work permit.

A Business Visa is required if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. E.g.; Meetings, Conference, Training Visas, Trade Fairs, etc; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within South Africa. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in South Africa he/she will need to obtain a work permit.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Download Form
You must submit an application for a visa  DHA 84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form, the same can also be accessed by click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2DqL7x1qcXLDohaDf37URm/d42d74c2ec9cf34b680317c119f33d9f/short-term-checklist.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
          
Application form should be filled in BLACK INK only.

visa_types.name: Relatives Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/xGPQQe97rOAfuMXLBQiKD/fef237f8e514cd1d67239d00cc59550c/Relatives.pdf) to download documents required checklist
An applicant for a relative's visa shall submit-
- A police clearance certificate from all the Countries he/she lived in since the age of 18yrs for 12 months or longer;(must not be older than 6 months at the time of its submission)
- Proof of kinship,
- An unabridged birth certificate; and
- Where necessary, paternity test results.
	The financial assurance contemplated in section 18(1) of the Act shall be an amount, per person and per month, as determined from time to time by the Minister by notice in the Gazette, to be proven by means of a current salary advice or a certified bank statement not older than three months at the time of application: 

Provided that the financial assurance shall not be required where the South African citizen or permanent resident is a dependent child. 

	A relative's visa may be issued for a maximum period of two years at a time.
- Medical and radiological report in respect of applicant and all members accompanying him/her, except pregnant women and children younger than 12 years
- A vaccination certificate, if required by the act (Yellow Fever)
- Duly completed application form signed by the applicant
- Two Passport Photographs
- Copy of the Passport (Bio Page)
- No fee – first level of kinship(i.e spouses and children of citizens/ permanent residence holders)
Processing of relatives visa takes a maximum of 8 weeks

visa_types.overview:
Overview
   As an immediate family member of a South African citizen or permanent resident you may be issued a relative's permit once the financial means as contemplated in the Immigration Act, 2002 (Act No. 13 of 2002) have been complied with. Relative’s permits are valid for a minimum period of 24 months and may be extended
Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete:
We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Visa fees, Documents required, Photo specifications sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.

The bank will then generate a deposit slip which will be presented to the VFS Mombasa office along with all other supporting documentation and Application Form(s). Please make 2 photocopies of the bank deposit slip as part of the documentation.

## Documents Required
- Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/xGPQQe97rOAfuMXLBQiKD/fef237f8e514cd1d67239d00cc59550c/Relatives.pdf) to download documents required checklist
An applicant for a relative's visa shall submit-
- A police clearance certificate from all the Countries he/she lived in since the age of 18yrs for 12 months or longer;(must not be older than 6 months at the time of its submission)
- Proof of kinship,
- An unabridged birth certificate; and
- Where necessary, paternity test results.
	The financial assurance contemplated in section 18(1) of the Act shall be an amount, per person and per month, as determined from time to time by the Minister by notice in the Gazette, to be proven by means of a current salary advice or a certified bank statement not older than three months at the time of application: 

Provided that the financial assurance shall not be required where the South African citizen or permanent resident is a dependent child. 

	A relative's visa may be issued for a maximum period of two years at a time.
- Medical and radiological report in respect of applicant and all members accompanying him/her, except pregnant women and children younger than 12 years
- A vaccination certificate, if required by the act (Yellow Fever)
- Duly completed application form signed by the applicant
- Two Passport Photographs
- Copy of the Passport (Bio Page)
- No fee – first level of kinship(i.e spouses and children of citizens/ permanent residence holders)
Processing of relatives visa takes a maximum of 8 weeks

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown. 
	The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.) 
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes. 
 	Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind 
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows. 
The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined 
 	Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

You must submit an application for a visa 
DHA-1738 FORM 8 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/htoeWNamEIAJnLdzdJs1b/a3a256756f27b32795464ee1fc25c05f/1738-010716_form.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the Checklist.

Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
1 recent passport photograph taken against a white background. If you wear glasses, the photograph must show clearly with no flash reflection of the glasses(35mm * 45mm) (not more than 12 months old).
A passport or travel document valid for no less than 90 days after the expiry of your intended visit.
Copy of bio data page and original passport valid for 60 days and at least 2 free pages on passport
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only)
The signature on the form should be exactly the same as on the passport
There is no Visa application fee for Kenyan passport holders staying in South Africa for less than 30 days. Although a service charge of KES 5840 is applicable to be paid via M-Pesa at the Bank located inside the Visa Application Centre located at West One building 2nd floor Parklands.
A copy of valid Yellow Fever Card with signature and cover page which shows validity dates. (Mandatory) (make sure yellow fever vaccination card is valid for 10 years)
Introduction/recommendation letter from an employer, where applicable, signed with contact details, (full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers).
 Signed referral letter from a Doctor in Kenya specifying the nature of health problem. (Landline number as well as the physical address). 
Signed appointment letter from a Doctor in South Africa with contactable details of the doctor, (Landline number as well as the physical address).
Verifiable return flight booking
Confirmed Hotel reservation
If you have children (minors) travelling with you or joining in South Africa, you will need to provide the following additional documents:-
In case of minors or dependents the person covering the expenses should include and attach in the documents of the minor or dependents a financial or responsibility term and the copy of the identification - ID. 
MINOR APPLICANTS:Responsibility term/letter from the parents authorizing the minor of travels to South Africa and copy of the parents ID’s and copy of the Birth Certificate of the child (Required even if the parent is traveling with minor)
The signature of the form on the minor should be done by any one of the parents. 
- Proof of guardianship or custody or a detail Birth Certificate
- Consent from the guardian in the case of an unaccompanied minor
Proof of financial means to pay for your living expenses while in South Africa in the form of: 
- Sufficient funds in form of either a recent bank statement, for the last three months signed and stamped by the bank.
- Verifiable return flight booking
- Undertakings by the host(s) in South Africa
- Applicant must have VISA of the neighboring country where they will be transiting to.
Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths. Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. Third country destination visa is a must.
All above mentioned relevant documents need to be brought in originals for verification at the Centre with subsequent photocopies. Only Photocopies will be taken along with the application, giving originals back to the applicant excluding the bank statements. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required .
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1GiUo18EncJGVGvyowxggI/4b1a2c3a59e7726cb21809b62869dcc7/medical-treatment-checklist.pdf)

visa_types.documents_required:
Documents required to apply for a South Africa visa
Visa Application Form duly completed and signed by each applicant (DHA-84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) (To be filled in Black Ink Only) 
A valid passport in respect of each applicant, and the applicable fee, with at least 2 clear pages . Passport must be valid for a period of one month after departure from the Republic of South Africa.
A photocopy of a valid Yellow Fever Certificate –with signature and cover page.
Photocopy of bio-data page of your current passport and immediate previous if submitting a new one on application date. 
Verifiable return flight booking OR itinerary of country to country if travelling by road.
Confirmed hotel reservations. 
Passport size photograph(45x35mm)(not more than 12 months old).  Please visit next page for more information on photograph specifications. 
Signed confirmation of employment/study letter from an employer/Institution, with contact details, (full names of the employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers)
For self-employed applicants, letter from their Business, Certificates of Registration documents/Ownership with current contact address. If retired, please submit a written statement. Proof of occupation is mandatory
Non-refundable fee of Kshs. 4,800.00(to Countries that are not fee exempt).  This excludes VFS Service Fee.
For foreign nationals, you are required to have a work, study of residence permit valid for one year and above Endorsed on your passport. 
Signed Introduction/Recommendation Letter from an employer, where applicable, with contact details, (Full names of the Employers, approved signatories, physical address and phone numbers) stating purpose and period of visit.  Signed email attachments and faxes are acceptable. 
A signed letter of invitation/confirmation of training/conference, etc, from the inviting host with full details of the Company’s authorized representative; physical address and phone numbers(landline number is compulsory). 
Sufficient funds in form of  ORIGINAL  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Accompanying Children 
In addition to the requirements for the specific purpose of travel, Accompanying Children under 18 years will be required to provide:
Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) – Consent for persons under the age of 18 to travel to or from the Republic of South Africa. Please print Parental Consent Affidavit and have it signed by a Commissioner of Oaths.  Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of  a  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

Please read General Notes (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2wlR3qiEaReMf0fKqOmfmE/e695f660b6526b3f83856991de36cbfa/new-child-regulation.pdf) for further guidelines.  A copy of the certified Consent will be presented alongside all other supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre on application date. 
Sufficient funds in form of  a  recent bank statement for the last three(3) months signed and stamped by the  bank. 
Birth Certificate for Minors/Children travelling with parents.  Where applicable, Consent letter/s with  copy/s of parent/s identification card/passport data page hold be attached to the application. 
Where applicable, a copy of a court order granting the applicant parental responsibilities and rights in respect of the child
A letter from the person who is to receive the child in the Republic, containing his or her residential address in the Republic where the child will be residing. 
Where the application is for the attendance of an activity or event, include a letter from the Organization under whose control the activity or event will take place, confirming such attendance and whether or not the foreigner will be remunerated, and if remunerated, the amount of the remuneration. 
If the child is a student, attach a document from their learning institution. 
South Africa Mission reserve the right to request for any further documents /information if it is required so.
To print the checklist of documents required please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KfvcCuw8LEECveCTiiLwo/bdfdea0a380eca4635ddc29597ef1f53/New-short-term-checklist.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Download Form
You must submit an application for a visa  DHA 84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4B32nZu41dWMXgEeUMv9MM/58c67f45f5a0384a4990d7288eb9b870/new-application-form-short-term-dha-84.pdf) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form, the same can also be accessed by click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2DqL7x1qcXLDohaDf37URm/d42d74c2ec9cf34b680317c119f33d9f/short-term-checklist.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
          
Application form should be filled in BLACK INK only.

An application for a general work visa shall be accompanied by-
-  Proof of acknowledgment (MANDATORY) from the Department of Labour confirming that-
- A duly completed application form signed by the applicant (
DHA-1738 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/htoeWNamEIAJnLdzdJs1b/a3a256756f27b32795464ee1fc25c05f/1738-010716_form.pdf)
) (BLACK INK ONLY)
- A Current passport photograph (45x35)
- An official letter confirming provisional acceptance or acceptance at that learning institution and the duration of the course;
- An undertaking by the Registrar or Principal of the learning institution to:
Provide proof of registration as contemplated in the relevant legislation within 60 days of registration; or
	In the event of failure to register by the closing date, provide the Director-
General with a notification of failure to register within 7 days of the closing date of registration; 
- Within 30 days of de-registration, notify the Director-General that the applicant is no longer registered with such institution; and

from the parent or legal guardian who has been issued with a court order granting full or specific parental responsibilities and rights or legal guardianship of the learner;

)  or Current Salary Advice (Pay slip)

- A copy of a Valid Yellow Fever Certificate
- NOTE: 
- Compliance certificate mandatory for aviation schools(
)
